sphincs_plus_hypertree_pruning.highlight: Conduition presented 'hypertree pruning': trades SPHINCS+ signature budget for faster KeyGen/signing, with no verifier changes required
Maximal root-tree pruning yields ~500x faster KeyGen (SLH-DSA-128S); budget drops from 2^64 to 2^55
Balanced pruning (equal pruning across all layers) is optimal for signing speedup; halving keys halves signing and KeyGen time
Key drawbacks: pruned/unpruned keys are incompatible; reuse across pruning modes risks forgery; observable fingerprinting of pruned wallets
Primary use case: constrained hardware wallets (Ledger/Trezor take minutes for SLH-DSA); acceptable budget reduction for low-frequency signers

el_cl_pq_algorithm_split.highlight: EL and CL will NOT use the same PQ algorithm: CL can use stateful XMSS; EL requires stateless scheme (SPHINCS+)

organizational.highlight: One presentation cancelled/postponed; next call in two weeks
Recommended viewing: Justin Drake and Danbon panels on quantum timelines from ETHPrague and ETH Summit (videos available)

action_items.action: Conduition to implement hypertree pruning in software wallet and engage Ledger/Trezor on hardware wallet integration

decisions.decision: CL uses stateful XMSS; EL uses stateless SPHINCS+-based scheme — confirmed separate PQ algorithms per layer
